macro_events.event_summary: US government begins issuing tariff refunds on Monday, with import-heavy retailers positioned to receive significant cash paybacks.
primary_drivers: Direct cash refund inflows from previously paid tariffs boosting FCF and EPS
Margin recapture for retailers with high China/import COGS exposure
Sentiment rotation into tariff-loser names as the overhang reverses

beneficiaries.rationale: Five Below sources ~60%+ of merchandise from China at low price points; tariffs were a direct margin headwind and refunds translate to material per-share cash recovery given the small market cap.
Dollar Tree's $1.25-$7 price architecture was squeezed hardest by China tariffs; heavy import mix means refund checks are meaningful vs. operating income base.
Best Buy consumer electronics COGS is heavily China-sourced (TVs, laptops, appliances); tariff paybacks are a direct cash windfall on a large import base.
Williams-Sonoma furniture/home goods imports carried Section 301 tariffs; refunds recover margin on a high-ticket import category.
RH furniture sourced from Asia faced steep tariff costs that compressed gross margins; refund offers both cash and a sentiment reversal on a heavily shorted name.
YETI drinkware manufactured heavily in China was a named tariff loser; refund recovers prior paid duties directly to the P&L.
Walmart is the largest US importer by volume; absolute refund dollars are huge but diluted across a $600B+ revenue base, so per-share impact is smaller than pure-play importers.

headlines: Tariff refunds begin on Monday. These retailers are due big paydays
